title.en: JSON & CSV: Reading and Writing Data Files
title.ta: JSON & CSV: தரவுக் கோப்புகளைப் படித்தல் மற்றும் எழுதுதல்

description.en: Master json.loads/dumps for JSON data and csv reader/writer for tabular data — the two most common data formats.
description.ta: JSON தரவுக்கு json.loads/dumps, அட்டவணை தரவுக்கு csv reader/writer — இரண்டு மிகவும் பொதுவான தரவு வடிவங்களில் தேர்ச்சி பெறுங்கள்.

story_variants.en:
## Vaathiyaar's Data Warehouse

Vaathiyaar walks through a data warehouse. "Every API returns JSON. Every spreadsheet exports CSV. If you can read and write these two formats, you can talk to almost any system in the world!"

### JSON vs CSV

| Feature | JSON | CSV |
|---------|------|-----|
| Structure | Nested, hierarchical | Flat, tabular |
| Types | strings, numbers, bools, nulls, arrays, objects | Everything is a string |
| Use case | APIs, configs | Spreadsheets, databases |
| Python module | `json` | `csv` |

### Key Functions

| Function | Direction | Purpose |
|----------|-----------|----------|
| `json.dumps(obj)` | Python -> JSON string | Serialize |
| `json.loads(s)` | JSON string -> Python | Deserialize |
| `json.dump(obj, f)` | Python -> JSON file | Write to file |
| `json.load(f)` | JSON file -> Python | Read from file |

> **Key Concept:** `dumps` and `loads` work with **strings**. `dump` and `load` (no 's') work with **files**. The 's' stands for 'string'!

explanation.en: Import the json module for JSON operations
A Python dictionary to convert to JSON
dumps = dump to String. indent=2 for pretty printing
Pretty-printed JSON string
loads = load from String. Converts JSON back to Python dict
Access data like a normal Python dictionary
Open a file for writing JSON
dump (no 's') writes directly to a FILE
Open the file for reading
load (no 's') reads from a FILE
Same dictionary we started with
Import the csv module for CSV operations
Open for writing — newline='' prevents double line breaks
Create a CSV writer object
Write the header row
Write multiple data rows at once
Open the CSV for reading
DictReader treats first row as headers — each row is a dict!
Iterate through rows as dictionaries
Access columns by header name — very clean!

animation_sequence.code:
import json

data = {'name': 'Alice', 'age': 30, 'skills': ['Python', 'SQL']}
json_str = json.dumps(data, indent=2)
print(json_str)

parsed = json.loads(json_str)
print(f"Name: {parsed['name']}")
print(f"Skills: {parsed['skills']}")

executionSteps.description: dumps = dump to String, indent=2 for pretty print
JSON string with double quotes (not single)
loads = load from String, back to Python dict
Access parsed data like normal dict

instruction.en: Create a list of 3 product dictionaries with 'name' and 'price' keys. Write them to a JSON file, then read back and print the most expensive product.
instruction.ta: 'name' மற்றும் 'price' keys உடன் 3 product dictionaries பட்டியல் உருவாக்குங்கள். JSON file-ல் எழுதி, மீண்டும் படித்து மிகவும் விலையுயர்ந்த product-ஐ அச்சிடுங்கள்.

practice_challenges.starter_code:
import json

products = [
    {'name': 'Laptop', 'price': 999},
    {'name': 'Mouse', 'price': 29},
    {'name': 'Keyboard', 'price': 79}
]

with open('products.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(products, f, indent=2)

with open('products.json') as f:
    loaded = json.load(f)

expensive = max(loaded, key=lambda p: p['price'])
print(f'Most expensive: {expensive["name"]} (${expensive["price"]})')

en: Use max() with a key function to find highest price
lambda p: p['price'] extracts the price for comparison
ta: max()-ஐ key function-உடன் பயன்படுத்தி அதிக விலை கண்டுபிடியுங்கள்
lambda p: p['price'] ஒப்பிடுவதற்கு விலையை எடுக்கிறது

q.en: What is the difference between json.dump and json.dumps?
q.ta: json.dump மற்றும் json.dumps இடையே என்ன வேறுபாடு?

en: dump writes to a file, dumps returns a string
dumps writes to a file, dump returns a string
ta: dump file-ல் எழுதுகிறது, dumps string திருப்புகிறது
dumps file-ல் எழுதுகிறது, dump string திருப்புகிறது

q.en: What does csv.DictReader do differently from csv.reader?

en: Returns each row as a dictionary with header keys
ta: ஒவ்வொரு row-ஐயும் header keys உடன் dictionary-ஆக திருப்புகிறது

q.en: Why use newline='' when opening a CSV file for writing?
q.ta: CSV file-ஐ எழுத open செய்யும்போது newline='' ஏன் பயன்படுத்த வேண்டும்?

en: To prevent double newlines on Windows
